status: DRAFT — NOT PUBLISHED — SME REVIEW REQUIRED
note: DRAFT end-of-course assessment authored for RA-7001. Every question is grounded in this course's newly authored lesson bodies (data/wordpress-export/courses.json) and paraphrases/applies ANSI/IICRC S520:2024 with clause citations — it does not reproduce the Standard's copyrighted text. Australian English, metric units and AS/NZS context. sourceRef is a review aid only and is not consumed by the seed script. DO NOT seed or publish until a qualified subject-matter expert has reviewed both the course content and these questions for technical accuracy. Structured identically to data/seed/floor-care-quizzes.json. This course carries 5 IICRC CEC hours.

questions.questionText: S520:2024 §8 is titled Limitations, Complexities, Complications and Conflicts. What is its relevance to a Level 3 job?
options: It lists the chemicals approved for large jobs
It frames the factors — large or hidden contamination, susceptible occupants, disputes, Category 3 water — that escalate a job beyond a standard remediation
It sets the maximum price a remediator may charge
It is only relevant to residential jobs

questions.questionText: Full containment for a complex job typically includes which of the following?
options: An open work area with a fan pointing outward
Critical barriers over openings, an anteroom/decontamination chamber, and continuous negative pressure
A single sheet of plastic taped to one wall
Positive air pressure to push air out of the room

questions.questionText: Why should the pressure differential of a full containment be monitored and logged (for example with a manometer)?
options: To calculate the electricity bill
To be able to demonstrate that negative pressure — and therefore containment — was maintained for the duration of the works
It is not necessary on any job
To measure the outdoor temperature

questions.questionText: In Australian construction, which locations are common hiding places for hidden/systemic mould on a large job?
options: Only the visible face of interior walls
Wall cavities behind Gyprock, the sub-floor, the roof void and behind sarking, plus double-brick cavities and party walls
Only inside light fittings
Mould never hides inside building assemblies

questions.questionText: Under S520:2024 §11, what is the correct first step when handling contents in affected areas?
options: Dispose of everything immediately
Inspect and evaluate each item for restorability, then inventory and pack items removed from affected areas
Clean everything with bleach on site
Return all items to the occupant untouched

questions.questionText: How should high-value and irreplaceable items (artworks, documents, heirlooms) be handled?
options: Treated exactly like ordinary contents
Given special care and, where appropriate, specialist referral, per §11.4
Always discarded as unrestorable
Left in the contaminated area

questions.questionText: A ducted HVAC system serves a large affected building. Why is it a particular concern on a Level 3 job?
options: It cannot spread contamination
It can distribute spores building-wide, so it must be isolated, assessed and, where required, cleaned; Australian mechanical ventilation is designed to AS 1668.2
It is always outside the remediation scope
It should be run continuously to dry the building

questions.questionText: Why should the Indoor Environmental Professional who performs independent clearance not be the party that did the remediation?
options: To increase the total invoice
Because independence — the verifier being different from the remediator — is the point of an arm's-length clearance
Because remediators are not allowed to see the results
It makes no difference who verifies

questions.questionText: You uncover material likely to contain asbestos in a pre-2004 building during mould works. Under S520:2024 §8.5 and §8.9, what must you do?
options: Continue the mould works and remove the asbestos yourself
Stop the mould work and engage licensed removalists under the relevant WHS asbestos regulations before continuing
Cover it with plastic and keep working
Ignore it because mould is the priority
questions.sourceRef: Course module 7; S520:2024 §8.5, §8.9; WHS asbestos regulations

questions.questionText: Which items form the litigation-grade documentation package expected on a complex mould project?
options: Only the final invoice
Written scope and work plan, photographic evidence, pressure and moisture logs, waste records, change orders, and any deviation with its rationale
A verbal handover to the site manager
The technician's personal notes, kept privately

questions.questionText: For how long should project records be handled after close-out?
options: They can be deleted as soon as the invoice is paid
They should be retained for the required retention period per §6.4
Only until the occupant returns
Records retention does not apply to mould work

questions.questionText: Large projects involve owners, occupants and insurers whose interests can conflict (§8.4). What is the best practice for managing this?
options: Avoid communicating to prevent disagreement
Communicate health concerns and process honestly, manage expectations about what verification can prove, and keep approvals in writing
Let the insurer make all technical decisions
Promise occupants a spore-free result

questions.questionText: What marks the true completion of a Level 3 project?
options: When the visible mould is gone
When independent verification confirms a return to Condition 1 and the documentation/evidence trail is complete
When the containment is taken down
When the final coat of paint is applied
